qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated /files/images route bypasses flow ownership checks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a missing ownership check in `GET /api/v1/files/images/{flow_id}/{file_name}` under `api/v1/files.py`, while the advisory is a `/validate/code` remote code execution issue in `validate_code()` handling of `exec_globals` and `exec()`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated webhook route executes non-webhook flows
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an authorization and webhook-guard failure in `POST /api/v1/webhook/{flow_id_or_name}` under `api/v1/endpoints.py`, not the advisory's `POST /validate/code -> validate_code() -> exec()` root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's `/validate/code` `validate_code()` `exec_globals`/`exec()` remote code execution path; the session's qualifying findings are separate broken-access-control issues in the files and webhook routes.
evidence: GHSA-g22f-v6f7-2hrh describes Langflow remote code execution in the validate endpoint through `validate_code()` handling of `exec_globals` and `exec()`.
The vulnerable validate implementation in `.judge/advisory_repo/src/lfx/src/lfx/custom/validate.py` compiles top-level `FunctionDef` nodes and executes them with `exec(code_obj, exec_globals)`.
`files_images_auth_bypass` instead concerns `GET /api/v1/files/images/{flow_id}/{file_name}` reading image bytes without the `get_flow` ownership check used by `/download`.
`webhook_non_webhook_flow_execution` instead concerns `POST /api/v1/webhook/{flow_id_or_name}` scheduling non-webhook flows and running them as the owner when webhook auth is disabled.
